text:
BRUNSON'S PUB: ST. PAUL'S NEIGHBORHOOD FAVORITE

Overview
Brunson's Pub is a welcoming neighborhood restaurant and bar located on St. Paul's East Side. Since opening in 2017, the establishment has become a go-to spot for quality comfort food, craft cocktails, and good times on Payne Avenue.

Location & Hours
Address: 956 Payne Ave, St. Paul, MN 55130
Free parking available on-site.

Regular Hours:
- Sunday to Thursday: 11am - 11pm (Kitchen: 11am - 10pm)
- Friday & Saturday: 11am - 12 midnight (Kitchen: 11am - 11pm)

Brunch Service:
- Every Saturday & Sunday: 11am - 2pm
- Full menu also available during brunch hours

Cuisine & Menu Style
Brunson's serves scratch-made comfort food with a creative touch. Chef Torrance Beavers brings culinary expertise to classic American fare, creating approa

on's serves scratch-made comfort food with a creative touch. Chef Torrance Beavers brings culinary expertise to classic American fare, creating approachable yet flavorful dishes. The menu includes burgers, sandwiches, salads, and shareables.

Signature Dishes & Menu Highlights
- Scratch-made burgers with creative toppings and flavor combinations
- Po' Boy sandwiches (Catfish Po Boy is a rotating special)
- House-made dressings and sauces
- Wedge salad with smoked blue cheese, red onion, tomatoes, avocado, and bacon
- Add-ons include grilled chicken, brisket, or seared yellowfin tuna
- Weekly Chef's features (new specials every Wednesday)

Beverage Program
The pub features 19 taps with rotating craft beers, creative cocktails, and a curated wine list. Local breweries like Summit and Schell'

erage Program
The pub features 19 taps with rotating craft beers, creative cocktails, and a curated wine list. Local breweries like Summit and Schell's are regularly featured.

Special Events & Weekly Features
Tuesday: Trivia Mafia at 8:30pm with Schell's & Summit specials starting at 8:30pm, Late Night Happy Hour at 9pm.
Wednesday: Chef's Weekly Feature (new dish each week), Brunson's Wednesday Wing (one-day-only flavor drop - peppadew pepper jam, peanuts, and green onion variations), Wine & Whiskey Wednesday with rotating pours.
Sunday: Chopped Cheese Sundays (Harlem-inspired, St. Paul-perfected) available after 4pm.
Weekend: Brunch service with Lovejoy's Bloodies, fresh-squeezed mimosas, and scratch-made classics.

Happy Hour Schedule
Monday - Thursday: 2pm - 6pm
Friday: 2pm - 5pm
Late

with Lovejoy's Bloodies, fresh-squeezed mimosas, and scratch-made classics.

Happy Hour Schedule
Monday - Thursday: 2pm - 6pm
Friday: 2pm - 5pm
Late Night Happy Hour: Sun - Thu 9pm - 11pm, Fri & Sat 10pm - midnight
(Food specials run until kitchen closes; drink specials continue through late night hours)

Ordering & Accessibility
Online ordering available through ChowNow. No reservations needed - walk-ins welcome. Casual, neighborhood vibe perfect for students and locals.

Parking
Free parking lot available on-site.

Contact & Social Media
Facebook: @BrunsonsPub
Instagram: @brunsonspub
Website: www.brunsonspub.com
Online Orders: order.chownow.com

What Makes It Special
Brunson's combines quality food with neighborhood hospitality. Chef Torrance Beavers' creative touch elevates traditional

w.com

What Makes It Special
Brunson's combines quality food with neighborhood hospitality. Chef Torrance Beavers' creative touch elevates traditional comfort food beyond standard pub fare. The rotating weekly features and specials keep the menu fresh. The East Side location is convenient to Metro State and features authentic neighborhood character.

CASUAL DINING & BUDGET-FRIENDLY OPTIONS NEAR METRO STATE

Student Budget Reality
As a student at Metro State, finding quality food that doesn't break the bank is essential. The good news: St. Paul's East Side has abundant casual dining and budget-friendly options that don't compromise on quality or authenticity.

Lower price points ($8-15 per meal)
No reservations needed
Relaxed dress code
Friendly, unpretentious atmosphere
Often family-owned and flexible with special requests

EASTSIDE ST. PAUL RESTAURANTS: A GUIDE FOR METRO STATE STUDENTS

Understanding the East Side Food Scene
The East Side of St. Paul has become a culinary hotspot with diverse dining options ranging from casual neighborhood spots to upscale restaurants. This area is particularly accessible to Metro State University students and features authentic, community-rooted establishments.

What Makes East Side Special
The neighborhood combines historic character with contemporary culinary creativity. You'll find family-owned businesses serving traditional recipes alongside innovative restaurants pushing culinary boundaries. The area maintains authentic neighborhood feeling while attracting broader food lovers.

Neighborhood Character
Multi-cultural community with deep roots
Family-owned and operated e

eighborhood feeling while attracting broader food lovers.

FOOD CULTURE & DINING PHILOSOPHY: UNDERSTANDING THE ST. PAUL FOOD SCENE

Beyond the Menu: Why Food Matters
Food is more than sustenance. It's culture, tradition, community connection, and self-expression. The St. Paul East Side restaurant scene embodies this philosophy through family-owned businesses, authentic preparations, and genuine community relationships.

What Makes St. Paul's Food Scene Special
Immigrant communities preserving cultural traditions
Multi-generational family businesses
Affordable pricing that values accessibility
Authentic food that hasn't been "Americanized"
Real community relationships between regulars and owners
Food as cultural bridge and storytelling medium

The Philosophy of Authentic Dining

What Authentic Means
Food prepared using traditional methods
Recipes r

as cultural bridge and storytelling medium

The Philosophy of Authentic Dining

What Authentic Means
Food prepared using traditional methods
Recipes reflecting genuine cultural origins
Ingredients selected for authentic flavors (not convenience)
Pricing that reflects ingredient costs rather than premium branding

HMONG VILLAGE SHOPPING CENTER: ST. PAUL'S UNIQUE CULINARY DESTINATION

Overview
Hmong Village is a one-of-a-kind indoor market in the United States, located in the heart of Eastside St. Paul. This sprawling shopping center combines dining, grocery shopping, cultural goods, and professional services all under one roof, making it a central hub for Hmong and Southeast Asian communities.

Location
Heart of Eastside St. Paul, Minnesota
Convenient access for Metro State University students

What Sets Hmong Village Apart
Unlike typical shopping malls, Hmong Village features over 250 vendors representing authentic Hmong and Southeast Asian businesses. It's designed as an immersive cultural experience where you can taste authentic foods, shop for specialty groceries, and experience unique goods and

. It's designed as an immersive cultural experience where you can taste authentic foods, shop for specialty groceries, and experience unique goods and services.

Food & Dining Options
Food Court: Multiple dining options with 17 restaurants and/or delis
Fresh Groceries: On-site market with specialty ingredients
Authentic Southeast Asian Cuisine: Direct from food vendors with roots in Hmong and Southeast Asian culinary traditions

Restaurant & Deli Highlights
The food court features 17 different restaurants and delis, each offering authentic Hmong and Southeast Asian cuisine. Options include:
- Traditional pho and noodle dishes
- Regional specialties from Hmong cuisine
- Authentic Southeast Asian preparations
- Fresh ingredients and family recipes

alties from Hmong cuisine

SOUTHEAST ASIAN DINING NEAR METRO STATE: AUTHENTIC FLAVORS & TRADITIONS

Why Southeast Asian Cuisine Matters
St. Paul's East Side has become a destination for authentic Southeast Asian cuisine, particularly Hmong and Vietnamese traditions. These aren't touristy interpretations but genuine food cultures where recipes reflect generations of family tradition.

The Food Culture
Southeast Asian cuisine in St. Paul is deeply rooted in community immigration and family entrepreneurship. Restaurants and food vendors often represent first, second, and third-generation businesses continuing family recipes.

Slow-cooked broth simmered for hours (sometimes days)

Conclusion
Weekend brunch near Metro State offers something for every mood and budget. From casual community cafes to upscale dining, the East Side provides excellent options for creating weekend memories and supporting local businesses.
